title: What percentage of the efficient price is reflected in the retail price of fuel?
subtitle: By income group and fuel type
textParagraph: In high-income countries, retail prices constitute a relatively large share of efficient prices, reflecting higher taxation and stronger regulatory frameworks. Natural gas is relatively less harmful to the environment, so a significant share of the efficient price is covered by the retail price, even in low-income settings. By contrast, diesel is mostly harmful to the environment, so that retail prices largely fall short of efficient prices, especially in upper-middle-income countries.
source: Black, Liu, Parry, and Vernon (2023) [reference: black1]
visdescription: Across all income groups, the retail price of fuel is often a fraction of the efficient price. The gap is substantial in middle-income countries, especially upper-middle-income countries. The highest gap is observed for coal, followed by diesel. These are important sources of external costs, such as air pollution and global warming. In middle-income countries, the retail prices of coal and diesel should more than double to internalize all social costs.
description: Across all income groups, the retail price of fuel is often a fraction of the efficient price.
